add-new-agent-provider.intent: Show a future agent the exact contract and integration points for teaching regin about a new coding-agent CLI (Kimi/Codex-style): subclass the AgentProvider adapter, register it, and wire transcript parsing, hook install, and skill deployment so regin captures the agent's sessions without hard-coding vendor paths or payload shapes across the shared pipeline.
add-new-agent-provider.label: Adding a new agent provider (AgentProvider adapter playbook: contract, registry, hook/transcript wiring)

agent-memory.blurb: 记忆的捕获/蒸馏/存储/召回/反思/反馈/exemplar——任务涉及 memory 写入、recall、lesson 时进来。代码：lib/memory/（除 evaluate.py）
agent-memory.intent: 记忆的捕获/蒸馏/存储/召回/反思/反馈/exemplar——任务涉及 memory 写入、recall、lesson 时进来。代码：lib/memory/（除 evaluate.py）
agent-memory.label: Agent 记忆引擎

agent-memory-architecture.intent: Orient a future agent in regin's cross-session memory subsystem before touching any of it: the thin module-verb facade in `__init__.py` (`remember`/`recall`/`reflect`/`get`/`update`/`forget`/`supersede`/`stats`), the four ports in `ports.py` (`EmbeddingProvider`, `LLMProvider`, `MemoryStore`, `MemorySink`) that every concrete provider is injected behind so no module under `lib/memory/` imports a concrete service, the edge adapters in `adapters.py` (`SkillRouterEmbedding` bi-encoder+cross-encoder, `ExternalAgentLLM`/`resolve_distiller`), and the `scoping.py` policy layer that stamps `global` / `repo:<name>` onto writes and recall. The single mutable `memories` table carries both tiers (`working`/`episodic`) via a `tier` column with its side tables (`memory_embeddings`, `memory_validations`, `injection_events`, `memory_edges`, `memory_topics`, `memory_authoritative_topics`, `memory_exemplars`/`topic_exemplars`, `topic_injections`/`topic_route_decisions`, `referent_session_df`); `models.py` declares its own `MetaData` so `regin init`/`rebuild` and Alembic never see memory tables, and `engine.py` is a self-initializing third engine (`db/regin_memory.db`) that runs `create_all` plus FTS5 DDL and idempotent rename/column migrations on first checkout.
agent-memory-architecture.label: Agent memory architecture (ports/adapters engine, self-initializing DB, tiers & lifecycle model)

agent-providers.blurb: AgentProvider 适配器（Claude/Kimi）、transcript 解析、hook 安装——接新 coding-agent 时进来。代码：lib/providers/
agent-providers.intent: AgentProvider 适配器（Claude/Kimi）、transcript 解析、hook 安装——接新 coding-agent 时进来。代码：lib/providers/
agent-providers.label: Agent 适配层

data-config.blurb: ORM/SQLModel、db/schema.sql、settings、auth、多用户、sync——动 schema、设置、鉴权、同步时进来。代码：lib/orm/, lib/settings.py, lib/auth.py, lib/sync/
data-config.intent: ORM/SQLModel、db/schema.sql、settings、auth、多用户、sync——动 schema、设置、鉴权、同步时进来。代码：lib/orm/, lib/settings.py, lib/auth.py, lib/sync/
data-config.label: 数据与配置

eval-grading.blurb: LLM-judge 会话评分（rubric/correctness/coverage/grounding/agentic）、recall 质量评估、pattern 隐藏实验、goal 验证——任何度量质量/裁决好坏的事进来。代码：lib/grader/, lib/memory/evaluate.py, lib/experiments.py, lib/goal_feedback.py
eval-grading.intent: LLM-judge 会话评分（rubric/correctness/coverage/grounding/agentic）、recall 质量评估、pattern 隐藏实验、goal 验证——任何度量质量/裁决好坏的事进来。代码：lib/grader/, lib/memory/evaluate.py, lib/experiments.py, lib/goal_feedback.py
eval-grading.label: 评估与评分

hooks-injection.blurb: hook_plugin、UserPromptSubmit/PostToolUse 接线、prompt 模板注入——调 hook 行为或注入内容时进来。代码：lib/hook_plugin.py, lib/prompt_templates.py
hooks-injection.intent: hook_plugin、UserPromptSubmit/PostToolUse 接线、prompt 模板注入——调 hook 行为或注入内容时进来。代码：lib/hook_plugin.py, lib/prompt_templates.py
hooks-injection.label: Hook 与注入

aliases: how past lessons reach the prompt
memory-auto-injection.intent: Show how recalled memories actually reach an agent and why the delivery path is shaped the way it is: the `UserPromptSubmit` handler (`hook_manager/handlers/memory_recall.py`) is a fresh short-lived process per prompt, so it borrows the warm `regin serve` embedder via a loopback-auth-exempt POST to `/api/memory/recall` (`_recall_via_server`) and cleanly falls back to in-process FTS-only (`_recall_fts`); speculative inject never reinforces (overlap-gated and same-session deduped via `injection_events`, recorded by `record_injections`) except for the earned `reinforce_resurfaced`; each inject (and the routed `<topic_context>` banner) is recorded so the feedback loops can later judge it; and the long-lived `recall` MCP server (`mcp_server.py`) is the deliberate deeper-pull path. Also covers the `memory.recall` span (`_emit_recall_span`) emitted into the trace so a reviewer can see exactly what was fed to each prompt.
memory-auto-injection.label: Memory auto-injection & on-demand recall (UserPromptSubmit hook, recall MCP server, /api/memory/recall)

aliases: the sleep cycle of agent memory
attach synthesis cluster to topic graph
memory-consolidation-reflect.intent: Explain `reflect()` — the batch consolidation cycle that turns raw `working` rows into curated `episodic` knowledge — step by ordered step: near-duplicate dedup (`_dedup_and_judge`, embedding cosine or text-ratio fallback), LLM-judged contradiction in the similarity gray zone, working→episodic promotion with `recall_count`-driven importance, a pre-decay engagement densifying sweep (`_score_pending` calling `feedback.rebuild_session_referent_df` + `score_pending_sessions`), importance decay for chronically-ignored rows whose gate reads the soft-vs-hard engagement bits (`_decay_chronically_ignored`/`_engagement_spare`), LLM synthesis of higher-order rules from cosine clusters (`_synthesize`), content-hash-skipped embedding (`_embed_episodic`), forget-stale retirement of never-recalled aged rows, and optional filesystem stale-ref flagging. Also covers the by-products: the undirected `related`-edge graph (`_harvest_edges`), emergent `memory_topics`, and `topic_attach.py` proposing merge/create onto the human-approved `.regin/topics` graph.
memory-consolidation-reflect.label: Memory consolidation (reflect: dedup, contradiction, promote, synthesize, forget-stale, decay, edges & topics)

memory-curation-surfaces.intent: Point an agent at the human-facing controls for curating the store — the curation that is the entire point of memory being mutable. Covers the `/api/memory/*` blueprint (`web/blueprints/memory.py`: list, get, related, bulk, recall probe, run-reflect, topics, graph, approve, retire, PATCH update, DELETE forget, plus the exemplar add/remove/list/forget and topic-feedback decision + route-preview routes), the `MemoryView.vue` dashboard and its `frontend/src/components/memory/*` panels (list, detail, category bar, topics, topic feedback, route playground, exemplars, exemplar cases), and the `regin memory {recall,list,stats,reflect,distill,approve,forget,supersede,link-topics,topic-feedback,topic-decide,exemplar-*,eval}` CLI mirror — the three entry points for reviewing distill proposals, fixing veracity, hand-curating exemplars, and probing what a query would surface.
memory-curation-surfaces.label: Memory curation surfaces (Memory view, /api/memory/* blueprint, regin memory CLI)

aliases: implicit capture from a finished session
memory-distillation-capture.intent: Explain the two ways a memory is born and why distillation is LLM-only by contract: the explicit path (`send_to_user(type=lesson)` teed into the store by `post_tool_trace._remember_lesson` with span/agent/scope provenance, honoring a `supersedes` id), and the implicit agentic distiller (`distill.py::distill_session`) that reads a finished session's trace, is handed only the high-signal hints (`_grade_digest` grader findings + `_signal_digest` failure→fix / pushback chains) plus the read-only trace dump/span commands and self-fetches just the spans it needs so prompt size stays constant. Covers the self-scored importance band (`_finalize_status`: drop below `distill_min_importance`, auto-approve at/above `auto_approve_importance`, else queue as `proposed`), dedup-at-write reinforcement (`_dedup_candidate`/`_reinforce_existing`), and at-write contradiction supersede (`_supersede_candidate`, gated by `distill_supersede_on_conflict`) — the curation gate that keeps the store from filling with session-narrating noise.
memory-distillation-capture.label: Memory capture & distillation (send_to_user lesson tee + agentic post-session distiller)

aliases: did the injected memory actually help
referent appears in a later span
soft vs hard ignore decay signal
memory-engagement-feedback.intent: Explain the half of the recall loop that learns whether a speculatively-injected memory helped: `score_injection_usefulness` pulls concrete referents (file paths, backtick identifiers) out of each injected memory (`_referents`) and checks whether any appear in a tool span that fired *after* injection (`_post_injection_spans` — the ordering gate is load-bearing), abstaining when there are no referents. Covers the idf weighting that discounts corpus-ubiquitous referents (precomputed session document-frequency in `referent_session_df` via `rebuild_session_referent_df`, gated by `engagement_idf_min_weight`), the `engaged`/`matched` bits stamped once per `injection_events` row (`_stamp_event`) so the decay gate can tell a soft ignore from a hard one, the importance reward for engaged hits (`_reward_engaged`), the reflect-time `score_pending_sessions` densifying sweep, and how this positive signal both records query exemplars and pairs with reflect's negative decay half.
memory-engagement-feedback.label: Memory engagement feedback (inject→usefulness verdict, idf-weighted referents, decay gate coupling)

aliases: why does this memory sink for some prompts
thumbs up down on a recall result
memory-exemplar-rescore.intent: Explain regin's signed query-exemplar mechanism — the contextual, query-local half of recall re-ranking that complements the global, slow `importance` decay. An exemplar is the embedding of a prompt a memory (or topic banner) was injected on, tagged `polarity` +1 (engaged) or -1 (hard ignore) and `source` `auto` (feedback-captured) or `manual` (human-curated); the unified `memory_exemplars` / `topic_exemplars` tables keep them per (memory|topic, model, polarity), each polarity capped at `negative_max_per_memory`. At recall time `store._apply_exemplar_rescore` multiplies a candidate's score by `clamp(1 − w_neg·max_cos(query, negatives) + w_pos·max_cos(query, positives), 0.05, exemplar_boost_ceil)`, so a memory injected-then-ignored on similar prompts sinks for *those* queries only and one proven useful is rescued when the cross-encoder under-ranks it — both fading once the exemplars stop resembling the incoming query, with nothing written to the memory row. Covers the auto write path (`feedback._record_exemplars` → `add_query_negatives`/`add_query_positives`, gated by `negative_demotion_weight`/`positive_boost_weight`), the manual curate path (`/api/memory/exemplars`, `MemoryExemplars.vue`, `regin memory exemplar-*`), and the inspect/per-row revert surfaces. The topic-route side of the same primitive is covered by `memory-topic-route-feedback`; the design note is `docs/agent-memory-exemplars.md`.
memory-exemplar-rescore.label: Signed query exemplars (contextual recall rescore + topic-route suppression, auto & hand-curated)

aliases: how a memory is scored and ranked
hybrid retrieval over the memory corpus
memory-recall-pipeline.intent: Explain the read-side ranking stack a `recall()` request flows through in `store.py`, so an agent debugging or tuning recall knows which knob moves which result: a `pattern_router`-shaped hybrid retrieve (`_lexical_ids` FTS5/BM25 leg + `_dense_ids` brute-force-cosine over `memory_embeddings` with `_lazy_backfill` + `_rrf` fusion + `_order_candidates` cross-encoder rerank), each stage degrading to the next without an embedder so an FTS-only path always works; the bounded `_quality_factor` ([0.9,1.3], gated by `recall_quality_weighting`) that re-ranks proven/recent memories by importance, veracity, `recall_count`, and recency without overriding relevance; `_apply_topic_boost` for memories linked to an authoritative topic node; optional `_mmr_select` diversity (`inject_mmr_lambda`) and opt-in 1-hop `_expand_via_edges` (`recall_expand_*`); the `expand.py` LLM query rewrite front-end for terse prompts; and `_bump_recall` reinforcement on deliberate recall. Measured by the FTS-only `evaluate.py` regression harness. The contextual exemplar rescore stage that also runs here has its own topic.
memory-recall-pipeline.label: Memory recall pipeline (FTS + dense + RRF + rerank, quality weighting, MMR, edge expansion, query expansion)

memory-topic-route-feedback.intent: Explain the relevance feedback loop wrapped around authoritative-topic routing — the topic analog of memory engagement feedback. The `UserPromptSubmit` hook records each injected `<topic_context>` banner as a `TopicInjection` row; the grader's `InjectedRelated` aspect (`config/settings.json` + `lib/grader/service._maybe_apply_injection_relevance`) judges whether the banner fit the user's goal and `store.apply_topic_relevance` stamps a `satisfied`|`needs_revision`|`fail` verdict once per session. Aggregated per topic (`topic_relevance_summary`), a recurring fail rate over `topic_relevance_min_scored` / `topic_relevance_fail_rate` only *proposes* suppression — withholding is human-in-the-loop: a `TopicRouteDecision` of `suppressed` makes the hook's `_route_topic` withhold the banner, `allowed` pins it on, and no row means `auto`. A `fail` also becomes a -1 topic exemplar and a `pass` a +1, so `store.topic_route_suppressed` can withhold query-locally when the incoming prompt's cosine to a topic's negatives clears `topic_negative_suppress_sim` unless a positive protects it. The route playground (`store.topic_query_signals` → `/api/memory/topic-route-preview`, `TopicRoutePlayground.vue` / `MemoryTopicFeedback.vue`) probes what a query would route to and why.
memory-topic-route-feedback.label: Topic-route relevance feedback (InjectedRelated verdict, fail-rate proposal, human suppression gate, route playground)

aliases: how other users see my topics
multi-user-topic-sharing.intent: Explain how regin's standalone-per-user model lets one user's approved topics become visible to other users: what travels via git (topic.json + wiki/<id>.md), what stays local (topic.local.json overlay, snapshot DB, in-flight proposals), and the producer/consumer steps (promote -> pre-commit add -> teammate git pull -> post-merge `topics import` -> snapshot DB seeded -> routable & visible in WebUI). Also covers the shared-server alternative when cross-user proposal review is needed.
multi-user-topic-sharing.label: Multi-user topic sharing (how approved topics travel from one user to another)

ops-cli.blurb: cli commands、doctor、audit、activity_log、db_rebuild、token 计量、search——跑 CLI、诊断环境、查活动日志时进来。代码：cli/, lib/doctor.py, lib/tokens/
ops-cli.intent: cli commands、doctor、audit、activity_log、db_rebuild、token 计量、search——跑 CLI、诊断环境、查活动日志时进来。代码：cli/, lib/doctor.py, lib/tokens/
ops-cli.label: CLI 与运维

pattern-experiments.intent: Understand the experiment system that conceals selected H2/H3 sections of a pattern's SKILL.md at deploy time so rule-trigger rates can be compared against an unconcealed baseline. Covers the pure conceal filter, the experiments table and RuleTrigger.experiment_id linkage, the one-active-per-pattern lifecycle, the redeploy hook into skill_sync/skill_deployer, the baseline-vs-variant rollup endpoints, the Vue UI surfaces, and the gap between the ingest path and the rollup's variant column.
pattern-experiments.label: Pattern Conceal Experiments

patterns-skills.blurb: 本地 pattern 指南 + skill 部署/读取——写 pattern、部署 skill、改 skill 索引时进来。代码：lib/patterns/, lib/skills/
patterns-skills.intent: 本地 pattern 指南 + skill 部署/读取——写 pattern、部署 skill、改 skill 索引时进来。代码：lib/patterns/, lib/skills/
patterns-skills.label: 模式与技能

proposal-review-comments.intent: Document the inline review-comment system layered on top of a proposal run: how a thread is anchored (general / topic field / proposal summary / wiki range), what HTTP endpoints CRUD threads and comments, how the ORM stores them with revision pointers, how `format_review_feedback_for_prompt` injects open threads into the next regenerate, and how the auto-`addressed` sweep closes threads whose anchored content actually changed.
proposal-review-comments.label: Proposal review comments (feedback threads anchored to topics & wiki)

rule-engine-design.intent: Explain how regin loads, dispatches, and executes lint/rewrite rules: the engine-agnostic Protocol, the three-source registry precedence (explicit settings → legacy grit fallback → autodiscovered bundles), the three concrete adapters (Grit, Bundle, Radon), the PostToolUse hook that drives them per edit, the two in-tree example bundles under example/rule/, and the step-by-step playbook for adding a new engine. Use this when adding a new engine kind, packaging a new rule pack as a bundle, wiring an existing engine into a new language, importing one of the example bundles, or debugging why a rule fired (or didn't) on a file.
rule-engine-design.label: Rule engine design (adapter protocol, registry, PostToolUse driver, in-tree example bundles, add-an-engine playbook)

rule-engines.blurb: GritQL/Radon/Bundle lint 引擎、语言适配、PostToolUse 强制——改 lint 规则或引擎时进来。代码：lib/rule_engines/, lib/rules/, lib/languages/
rule-engines.intent: GritQL/Radon/Bundle lint 引擎、语言适配、PostToolUse 强制——改 lint 规则或引擎时进来。代码：lib/rule_engines/, lib/rules/, lib/languages/
rule-engines.label: 规则引擎 (lint/rewrite)

session-trace.blurb: OTel 式 span 摄取、聚合、合并、trace UI 数据——改 trace 捕获/span 归属/聚合时进来。代码：lib/trace/
session-trace.intent: OTel 式 span 摄取、聚合、合并、trace UI 数据——改 trace 捕获/span 归属/聚合时进来。代码：lib/trace/
session-trace.label: 会话 trace

session-trace-design.intent: Explain how Claude Code sessions are captured as OpenTelemetry-style spans by hook handlers, persisted through the trace_service ingest layer into session_spans plus a denormalized sessions row, and rendered by the Vue SessionTraceView via the /api/sessions/* read endpoints.
session-trace-design.label: Session trace design (OTel-style spans, ingest pipeline, aggregates, dashboard read paths)

topic-graph.blurb: 主题分类树本身：路由、提案、review、多用户共享、wiki——改主题结构/路由/proposal 时进来。代码：lib/topics/
topic-graph.intent: 主题分类树本身：路由、提案、review、多用户共享、wiki——改主题结构/路由/proposal 时进来。代码：lib/topics/
topic-graph.label: 主题图与路由

topic-proposal-pipeline.intent: Explain the end-to-end funnel that turns a user's topic request into one row in `.regin/topics/topic.json`: an external tool-using agent drafts the JSON+wiki, regin validates and persists it via the ORM, reviewers iterate through revise/regenerate/restore/stop, and a single server-side /diff+/apply path (or the matching library accept/replace/merge helpers) promotes a topic into the approved graph. Also describes the cross-thread cancel machinery that lets a Stop request terminate the agent subprocess without racing the worker's terminal-state writes.
topic-proposal-pipeline.label: Topic proposal pipeline (request → draft → review → apply/stop)

topic-routing.intent: Understand how an agent resolves a freeform query into approved topic context: alias / label / ref-path / multi-keyword scoring against `.regin/topics/topic.json`, with optional fallback to unapproved candidates in `.regin/topics/candidates.json`. Covers the matcher (`match_topic`), the routing envelope (`route_topic`) that returns ordered refs + bounded wiki pages + first-degree related topics, and the deployed `topic-router` skill that tells agents how to use it.
topic-routing.label: Topic routing (approved graph + candidate fallback)

webui.blurb: Vue3 SPA 全部视图 + Flask /api blueprint——改前端页面或对应 API 时进来。代码：frontend/, web/
webui.intent: Vue3 SPA 全部视图 + Flask /api blueprint——改前端页面或对应 API 时进来。代码：frontend/, web/
webui.label: Web UI 与 API

aliases: what screens does the dashboard have
where is the view for this route
webui-surface.intent: Give a future agent a one-stop index of every page in the regin dashboard: which Vue view renders each route, how the routes are grouped in the sidebar, which feature flags hide a group, and how the SPA is built and served by Flask. It is a navigational map that hands off to the deep feature topics (memory, trace, rules, topics) rather than re-explaining them.
webui-surface.label: WebUI surface map (Vue 3 SPA pages, router routes, sidebar nav groups & the Flask shell that serves them)
